title: Template script creates project files as an import side effect
reasoning: All filesystem creation runs at module top level. Any test discovery, import, static analysis helper, or packaging step that imports `template.py` will create directories and empty files in the current working directory, which can pollute builds and developer worktrees.
reproduction: From a clean temporary directory with this file on `PYTHONPATH`, run `python -c "import template"`; it creates `backend`, `frontend`, `requirements.txt`, and `README.md` in the process working directory.
recommendation: Move the creation logic into a `main()` function and invoke it only under `if __name__ == '__main__':`. Consider requiring an explicit target directory argument.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests import `template.py` from an isolated working directory and assert that import is side-effect free.
suggestedRegressionTest: Import `template.py` in a temporary directory and assert no project files are created until `main()` is called explicitly.
minimumFixScope: Wrap the folder/file creation loops in `main()` and add a `__main__` guard.
